bridges.purpose: Short routing bridge that defers to canonical app knowledge

workflows.scope: template-compatible feature workflow routing
template-compatible data workflow routing
core PDF-to-DOCX translation behavior
schema/settings/checkpoint persistence and migration safety
localization terminology and routing contracts
workspace performance baselines and hygiene
external parity/inspiration discovery and adaptation
reference-locked Qt UI replication with deterministic render review
OCR-heavy translation troubleshooting and small-slice acceptance
parallel worktree baseline locking and build-under-test provenance
hard gate protocol for risk-triggered complex work
browser automation reliability with host/binary provenance
cloud-first heavy evaluation and local acceptance separation
official OpenAI docs citation and freshness governance
CI and repository operations
commit/push/publish safety and triage
assistant docs sync and drift control
project-local application of vendored assistant templates without editing the template folder
adaptive roadmap governance, resume anchor routing, and active-worktree continuity
same-host installation/auth/live-smoke preflight for local integrations
test isolation, listener ownership, and multi-surface diagnostics guidance for host-bound workflows

contracts.template_read_policy: docs/assistant/templates/* is read-on-demand only unless explicitly requested.
contracts.environment_outside_workspace_default_po: Keep heavyweight environments/artifacts outside workspace root when feasible.
contracts.post_change_docs_sync_prompt_policy: After significant implementation changes keep the exact prompt available: Would you like me to run Assistant Docs Sync for this change now? Ask it only when relevant touched-scope docs still remain unsynced and immediate same-task synchronization is necessary. If immediate same-task synchronization is not necessary, defer it to a later docs-maintenance pass. If the relevant docs sync already ran during the same task/pass, do not ask again.
contracts.inspiration_reference_discovery_policy: Named parity/inspiration requests must run REFERENCE_DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW before implementation decisions.
contracts.execplan_policy: Major/multi-file work requires ExecPlans in docs/assistant/exec_plans/active/.
contracts.approval_gates_policy: AGENTS.md and agent.md enforce ask-before-execute gates for destructive/risky actions.
contracts.worktree_isolation_policy: Parallel work uses git worktree isolation, approved baseline locks, and branch safety gates.
contracts.worktree_baseline_identity_policy: Parallel work must record worktree path, branch, base SHA, target integration branch, and build-under-test identity before major execution or GUI handoff.
contracts.doc_gardening_policy: Docs sync updates only touched-scope docs and avoids blanket rewrites.
contracts.project_harness_sync_policy: implement the template files and sync project harness apply vendored templates to this repo's local harness without editing docs/assistant/templates/*.
contracts.vendored_template_protection_policy: Vendored docs/assistant/templates/* files are committed project assets, not cleanup candidates, and require explicit template-system intent before any edits.
contracts.roadmap_dormant_main_policy: When roadmap governance is installed, main may intentionally carry a dormant roadmap state that explicitly says no active roadmap is currently open and routes normal work back to ExecPlan flow until roadmap mode is reactivated.
contracts.roadmap_resume_anchor_policy: When roadmap governance is active, docs/assistant/SESSION_RESUME.md is the roadmap anchor file and the stable first resume stop for resume master plan and equivalent fresh-session resume intents.
contracts.roadmap_active_worktree_authority_policy: During active roadmap work, the current worktree's SESSION_RESUME.md, active roadmap tracker, and active wave ExecPlan are authoritative for live roadmap state.
contracts.roadmap_update_order_policy: After roadmap changes or detours update the active wave ExecPlan first, the active roadmap tracker second, and docs/assistant/SESSION_RESUME.md third.
contracts.cleanup_complete_push_policy: Bare push means continuity-closeout plus cleanup: close completed ExecPlans, close roadmap state when active, update docs/assistant/SESSION_RESUME.md before merge, and remove known scratch outputs.
contracts.post_merge_repair_default_policy: If post-merge continuity or cleanup was missed, default to a follow-up branch/PR; direct repair on main requires explicit user intent.
contracts.scratch_root_default_policy: Deterministic assistant review/debug artifacts should default under ignored tmp/ unless a stricter local scratch root is documented.
contracts.user_guides_support_usage_policy: Support/non-technical tasks route to APP_USER_GUIDE and primary workflow user guide first.
contracts.user_guides_canonical_deference_policy: User guides are explanatory and defer to canonical technical docs.
contracts.user_guides_update_sync_policy: Major feature changes update relevant user-guide sections with matching technical docs.
contracts.template_path_routing_regression_protect: INDEX.md and validators enforce template-path routing and read policy protections.
contracts.issue_memory_policy: ISSUE_MEMORY.md and ISSUE_MEMORY.json capture repeated project issues for docs sync and generalized bootstrap maintenance decisions.
contracts.test_live_state_isolation_policy: Tests must default to temporary filesystem/environment state, explicit opt-in live machine state, and non-live or ephemeral ports for host-bound features.
contracts.multi_surface_diagnostics_packet_policy: For multi-surface workflows keep per-run artifacts primary, add workflow_context when a run belongs to a larger session, and use one durable app-owned session artifact instead of separate browser-only report files.
contracts.canonical_precedence_policy: Source code is final truth; APP_KNOWLEDGE.md is canonical; bridge/user guides defer to canonical.
contracts.docs_sync_prompt_policy: After significant implementation changes keep the exact prompt available: Would you like me to run Assistant Docs Sync for this change now? Ask it only when relevant touched-scope docs still remain unsynced and immediate same-task synchronization is necessary. If immediate same-task synchronization is not necessary, defer it to a later docs-maintenance pass. If the relevant docs sync already ran during the same task/pass, do not ask again.
contracts.stage_gate_policy: For risk-triggered complex work stop at each stage and require exact continuation token format NEXT_STAGE_X.
contracts.reference_discovery_policy: Parity/inspiration requests against named products/sites/apps require REFERENCE_DISCOVERY_WORKFLOW first.
contracts.openai_docs_citation_freshness_policy: OpenAI/external unstable claims must use official primary sources with explicit verification date in EXTERNAL_SOURCE_REGISTRY.md.
contracts.browser_automation_reliability_policy: Browser automation tasks require preflight capability checks, machine-first validation, and artifact packet output.
contracts.workspace_provenance_lock_policy: Browser automation tasks must declare canonical workspace path before execution.
contracts.automation_host_fallback_policy: Automation host failures must be classified as unavailable (tooling/env) or failed (assertion/flow).
contracts.machine_operator_split_validation_policy: Run deterministic machine checks first; reserve manual checks for perceptual outcomes.
contracts.restricted_browser_page_policy: Restricted browser pages must use normal-webpage/manual-input fallback when scripting is blocked.
contracts.browser_binary_strategy_policy: Prefer healthy existing browser first; use Chrome for Testing only when reproducibility/mismatch risk appears.
contracts.browser_app_primary_local_surface_policy: The local browser app on 127.0.0.1 in live mode is the preferred daily-use surface for this repo; Qt remains a supported secondary shell/fallback.
contracts.browser_app_live_mode_policy: Browser live mode uses the real settings, profiles, job log, outputs, and Gmail-linked workflows.
contracts.browser_app_isolated_shadow_mode_policy: Browser shadow mode is explicit isolated test mode with separate runtime roots, separate state, and no silent fallback into live data.
contracts.browser_gmail_bridge_owner_policy: When available, the browser app is the primary live Gmail bridge owner and the real extension opens or focuses the browser app workspace rather than relying on desktop-window focus helpers.
contracts.browser_extension_lab_companion_policy: Extension Lab is a browser diagnostics and simulation companion for the real Gmail extension, not a replacement for the extension.
contracts.template_sync_deferral_policy: Project docs must capture reusable browser live-vs-isolated mode patterns before any later template-folder sync, but template updates still require explicit template-system intent.
contracts.chromium_for_testing_conditional_install: Do not force browser install for API-only/non-UI tasks; allow conditional install only for automation reproducibility risk.
contracts.automation_binary_provenance_packet_poli: Automation packet must emit automation_browser_binary, automation_browser_version, automation_browser_source.
contracts.cloud_heavy_scoring_default_policy: Heavy machine evaluation runs default to cloud execution venue with local-only smoke/acceptance/apply checks.
contracts.cloud_scoring_preflight_gate_policy: Cloud evaluation requires dispatch/secrets/tooling preflight before heavy run start.
contracts.cloud_scoring_failure_recovery_policy: Cloud failure semantics use unavailable|failed and recovery order is cloud-fix-rerun before local-heavy fallback.
contracts.cloud_local_apply_separation_policy: Cloud evaluation outputs must not auto-apply runtime config/state; local manual acceptance gate is required before final apply.
